Benefits
Pulled from the full job description

Work from home
 
Full job description
Operations Manager

Salary: £45,000 per annum + performance-based incentives
Location: Remote
Hours: Monday to Friday, 8:00am to 5:00pm
On Call: Participation in on call rota, Escalation
Reporting to: Director

About the Role

We are looking for a driven and accountable Operations Manager to take ownership of day to day operational delivery across a fast growing national facilities management business.

This is not a passive management role. You will be responsible for driving performance, improving efficiency and ensuring jobs are delivered on time, to standard and profitably.

You will lead and manage account managers and engineers, control the operational diary, and take full responsibility for service delivery across reactive and planned maintenance.

You will also be expected to contribute to growth, improve processes and take ownership of results.

Key Responsibilities

Operational Delivery

Manage the operations diary and ensure efficient scheduling of engineers
Oversee allocation and completion of reactive maintenance jobs
Ensure planned maintenance is delivered on time and to standard
Maintain full oversight from job creation through to completion and invoicing
Ensure all jobs are completed professionally with accurate reporting
Team Management

Lead and manage account managers and operational staff
Work closely with supervisors to maintain control of delivery
Drive accountability, performance and discipline across the team
Conduct performance reviews and support staff development
Client & Service Delivery

Ensure high levels of service across all contracts
Act as escalation point for operational and client issues
Resolve complaints quickly and professionally
Protect and strengthen client relationships
Performance & Profitability

Take ownership of operational performance and efficiency
Ensure jobs are delivered profitably and resources are used effectively
Monitor and control overtime and call outs
Drive productivity across the team
Growth & Targets

Contribute to business growth and operational scaling
Meet performance, service and growth targets
Support sales delivery through strong operational execution
Financial rewards are aligned to performance and results
Systems & Technology

Operate and manage workflows through BigChange CRM
Use Freshdesk and internal systems for communication and ticketing
Work with Nirvana AI tools to improve efficiency and automation
Ensure accurate reporting and data integrity across systems
Compliance & Standards

Ensure compliance with operational and safety requirements
Maintain oversight of RAMS and documentation
Ensure all works meet required standards and certifications
What We Are Looking For

Strong experience in operations management within FM, maintenance or a similar fast paced environment
Proven ability to manage teams and drive performance
Commercial mindset with focus on profitability and efficiency
Highly organised with strong control of scheduling and workflow
Comfortable working in a high pressure, high accountability environment
Strong problem solver who takes ownership and delivers outcomes
Experience using systems such as BigChange or similar CRM platforms is preferred
Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

Pay: £45,000.00 per year

Benefits:

Work from home
Experience:

Engineer Diary Management: 6 years (required)
Managing a reactive maintenance helpdesk: 6 years (required)
Billing, Quoting, Scheduling, Running Operations: 6 years (required)
Work Location: Remote
shortRoleReason: Own day-to-day operational delivery, team performance, and profitability.
companySummary: Nirvana Maintenance is a fast-growing national facilities management business focused on reactive and planned maintenance delivery.
roleSummary: The Operations Manager will lead operational delivery, manage teams and workflows, improve efficiency, and ensure profitable, on-time service delivery.
headlineAttraction: Remote operations leadership role with performance-based incentives in a growing FM business.
rolePurpose: To take full ownership of operational delivery and improve performance, efficiency, and profitability across maintenance services.
advertSummary: Remote Operations Manager role at Nirvana Maintenance, reporting to the Director, responsible for operational delivery, team leadership, client service, profitability, compliance, and systems management across a fast-growing FM business.
personalisedIntro: I’m applying because this role sits right in the space where I do my best work: taking a busy operation, tightening control, and improving how it performs day to day. I’ve led operations in fast-moving, accountable environments where service quality, team discipline and financial outcomes all matter at once.
whyThisRole: The work itself fits the kind of responsibility I’ve held before: owning delivery, keeping workflows under control, and making sure the operation is both reliable and profitable. I’m comfortable with the pressure that comes with being the person who has to keep jobs moving, issues resolved and standards consistent.
companyPridePoints: Use of AI tools to improve efficiency

Oversee reactive maintenance job allocation and completion
Ensure planned maintenance is delivered on time and to standard
Maintain oversight from job creation through invoicing
Lead account managers, engineers, and operational staff
Work with supervisors to control delivery
Conduct performance reviews and support staff development
Monitor overtime, call outs, and productivity
Contribute to business growth and scaling
Use BigChange CRM, Freshdesk, and internal systems
Maintain reporting accuracy and data integrity
Ensure compliance with safety requirements, RAMS, and documentation

Commercial mindset focused on profitability and efficiency
Highly organised with strong control of scheduling and workflow
Comfortable in a high pressure, high accountability environment
Strong problem solver who takes ownership and delivers outcomes

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Operations leader who brings control, pace and commercial grip to complex service delivery.
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I’m applying because this role sits right in the space where I do my best work: taking a busy operation, tightening control, and improving how it performs day to day. I’ve led operations in fast-moving, accountable environments where service quality, team discipline and financial outcomes all matter at once.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: Nirvana Maintenance stands out to me because it is a fast-growing national business and the role is clearly tied to performance, not just oversight. I also like that the company is using AI tools and performance-based incentives, which suggests a practical focus on improvement and results.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: The work itself fits the kind of responsibility I’ve held before: owning delivery, keeping workflows under control, and making sure the operation is both reliable and profitable. I’m comfortable with the pressure that comes with being the person who has to keep jobs moving, issues resolved and standards consistent.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: operational control, team performance and profitability
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I led day-to-day operations during a scale-up phase, so I know what it takes to keep performance tight while the business is growing. The mix of delivery control, resilience and commercial improvement maps well to this role.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as the strongest proof of operational leadership and growth-phase control.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve led operations through growth while keeping delivery disciplined and commercially sound.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: service quality, escalation handling and customer satisfaction
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I improved patient experience in a live NHS setting where service follow-through really mattered, so I’m used to turning around poor perception through better operational control. That is relevant to a role where client service and complaint handling sit close to the centre of the job.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a proof point for service recovery and customer-facing accountability.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve turned around service perception by tightening follow-through and operational grip.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Ride compliance and operator progression tracking
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I built a practical tracking system in a safety-critical environment, which shows I’m comfortable putting structure around compliance and making it usable for the team. That matters here because the role depends on RAMS, documentation and consistent standards.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as supporting evidence for compliance mindset and process improvement.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve built simple control systems to keep compliance visible and manageable.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: I read this as a role that needs someone who can keep a busy maintenance operation under control without losing pace. It needs strong diary discipline, clear ownership of people and performance, and a commercial eye on overtime, productivity and profit.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: engine diary management and scheduling control
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve run day-to-day operations in a scale-up environment where timing, capacity and delivery discipline had to be managed closely.
experienceMappings.relevance: This shows I can keep work moving and maintain control when demand is changing.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: reactive service delivery and job completion
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve improved service follow-through in a live, high-pressure setting where issues had to be dealt with properly and quickly.
experienceMappings.relevance: That is close to the pace and accountability of reactive maintenance delivery.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led operational teams through growth and change, with a focus on disciplined execution and resilience.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role needs someone who can set expectations and keep people aligned.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: client escalation handling and complaint resolution
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked on improving external perception by tightening how service issues were handled and followed through.
experienceMappings.relevance: That translates well to protecting client relationships when things go wrong.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I contributed to margin improvement while the business scaled, which meant keeping a close eye on how the operation performed commercially.
experienceMappings.relevance: This role is explicitly tied to profitability, so that commercial discipline matters.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I created a tracking system to support compliance and operator progression in a safety-critical environment.
experienceMappings.relevance: That shows I’m comfortable putting structure around standards and making them workable.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve built live operational visibility tools to bring fragmented information into one place and make service strain easier to see.
experienceMappings.relevance: This supports the need for accurate reporting across systems and workflows.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve managed complex annual projects while maintaining service standards in a regulated, data-sensitive setting.
experienceMappings.relevance: The role calls for someone who is comfortable being accountable for outcomes.

focusAreasToBring.title: Operational control
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on keeping the diary, workflow and escalation points tight so jobs move cleanly from allocation to completion.

focusAreasToBring.title: Team discipline
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would set clear expectations, track performance properly and support managers and engineers to stay accountable.

focusAreasToBring.title: Commercial grip
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would keep a close eye on overtime, call-outs, productivity and the points where margin can leak.

focusAreasToBring.title: Service standards
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would make sure client issues are handled quickly, professionally and with proper follow-through.

focusAreasToBring.title: Systems and reporting
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would use the systems in place to improve visibility, data quality and day-to-day control.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: My background fits because I’ve spent my career in operations roles where delivery, people and performance all had to be managed together. I’ve worked in fast-moving, regulated and service-led environments, and I’m comfortable taking ownership when the expectation is to improve how the operation actually runs.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I would expect to spend my first months tightening operational control, improving visibility across scheduling and delivery, and making sure the team is working to clear standards. I’d also look for practical ways to reduce avoidable cost, improve response times and strengthen how issues are handled before they become client problems.
companyHighlights: Use of AI tools to improve efficiency and automation.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best where there is clear ownership, direct accountability and a focus on outcomes. That matches the way this role is described, and it suits how I like to operate.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would get close to the diary, job flow, escalation routes, reporting and team routines so I can see where delivery is strong and where it is leaking.
I would focus on scheduling discipline, workload visibility, overtime control and the practical rhythms that keep reactive and planned work on track.
I would look to lock in better reporting, clearer accountability and a few targeted process improvements that support service quality and profitability.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring a steady, hands-on approach to keeping the operation controlled, accountable and commercially sharp.
closingProofPoints: I improved Google reviews from 2.09 to 4.51 in NHS primary care.
I helped support a £149.9k improvement in profit before appropriation.
I built a compliance-tracking system in a safety-critical operation.
contentNotes: I have kept the evidence selection to three examples because they give the strongest spread across delivery control, service recovery and compliance.
I have not claimed direct FM or maintenance-sector experience because it is not present in the CV; the fit is based on transferable operational leadership in fast-paced environments.
The advert mentions BigChange and Freshdesk, but I have not claimed prior hands-on use because that is not supported by the CV.

examples.title: “Built live operational dashboard concept for GP practice visibility”
